skill.name: minimax-music-gen
skill.description: Use when user wants to generate music, songs, or audio tracks. Triggers on any request involving music creation, song writing, lyrics generation, audio production, or covers. Also triggers when user provides lyrics and wants them turned into a song, or describes a mood/scene and wants background music. Supports multilingual triggers — match equivalent phrases in any language. Do NOT use for music playback of existing files, music theory questions, or music recommendation without generation.
skill.summary: Generate original music and songs using the MiniMax AI music API.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: All 165 static analysis findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown documentation as executable code. Backtick detections are markdown fenced code blocks showing CLI usage examples. Cryptographic algorithm detections trigger on documentation words like 'API key', 'auth', and 'seed'. The skill is legitimate music generation documentation that instructs an AI assistant to use the mmx CLI tool for creating songs, covers, and instrumental tracks. No malicious patterns, data exfiltration, or security risks found.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI Tool
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI assistant to execute shell commands using the mmx CLI tool for music generation. Commands include mmx music generate, mmx music cover, mmx auth login, and mmx quota show. All command arguments are constructed from user input (prompts, lyrics, file paths) and passed through structured CLI flags. This is the intended functionality of the skill, not a security vulnerability. Users must install and authenticate the mmx CLI separately with their own API key.

low_findings.title: Credential Storage on Filesystem
low_findings.description: The mmx CLI stores API credentials in ~/.mmx/credentials.json as documented in SKILL.md line 41. This is standard behavior for CLI tools and is managed by the mmx tool itself, not by this skill. The skill documentation references this location so users know where credentials are stored. No credential access or manipulation is performed by the skill.

low_findings.title: System Program Detection
low_findings.description: The skill uses command -v to check for installed audio players (mpv, ffplay, afplay) at SKILL.md line 48 and line 245. This is standard practice for detecting available software and does not constitute system reconnaissance. The commands only check for the existence of specific media players needed for optional local playback.

content.user_title: Generate music and songs with AI
content.value_statement: Create original music, songs, and audio tracks without any production experience. This skill uses the MiniMax AI music API to generate vocal tracks, instrumentals, and covers from simple text descriptions.
actual_capabilities: Generate vocal songs with auto-optimized lyrics from a text description
Create instrumental tracks in any genre or style
Produce cover versions of songs from reference audio files or URLs
Support multilingual prompts and generate lyrics in multiple languages
Provide interactive control over lyrics, genre, mood, BPM, and instruments
Play generated audio through local media players
limitations: Requires the mmx CLI tool and a valid MiniMax API key for music generation
Generated output is limited to MP3, WAV, or PCM audio formats
Cannot play or process existing copyrighted music or lyrics
Reference audio for covers must be 6 seconds to 6 minutes and under 50MB
Requires Node.js for CLI installation and an audio player for local playback

use_cases.title: Create background music for content
use_cases.description: Content creators can generate custom background music for videos, podcasts, or presentations without needing music production skills or licensing existing tracks.

use_cases.title: Prototype songs and musical ideas
use_cases.description: Songwriters and musicians can quickly prototype melodies, lyrics, and arrangements to explore creative directions before committing to full production.

use_cases.title: Generate personalized gifts and soundtracks
use_cases.description: Individuals can create personalized songs as gifts or generate custom background soundtracks for events, games, or personal projects.

prompt_templates.title: Quick song generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a song: a melancholic piano ballad about rainy autumn evenings, with soft female vocals
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a complete song with minimal setup

prompt_templates.title: Instrumental track creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an upbeat 120 BPM electronic dance track with synth leads and driving bass for a workout playlist
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs background music for a specific activity

prompt_templates.title: Cover version with style change
prompt_templates.prompt: Make an acoustic cover of this song in a stripped-down, intimate folk style with gentle guitar
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to reimagine a song in a different genre

prompt_templates.title: Advanced multi-parameter song creation
prompt_templates.prompt:
I want a song with these lyrics:
[verse]
Walking through the city lights tonight
[chorus]
We are the dreamers of the neon glow

Style: 90 BPM neo-soul with warm Rhodes piano, brushed drums, and smooth emotional vocals. Make it feel like a late-night drive through the city.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has specific lyrics and detailed production requirements

output_examples.input: Make me a sad piano piece for studying
output: Description: melancholic piano, gentle tempo, soft dynamics, suitable for focused study
Confirm? (press enter to confirm, or tell me what to change)

output_examples.input: Create a cover of this audio file as a reggae version
output: Please provide the source audio file path or URL for the song you want to cover.
What style would you like for the cover? (e.g., acoustic, reggae, electronic)
Style: reggae version with off-beat guitar chops, warm bass line, relaxed vocal delivery

best_practices: Write vivid, descriptive prompts as narrative sentences rather than comma-separated tags for better generation quality
Always confirm the prompt preview with the user before starting generation to avoid wasting API quota
Use structured flags such as genre, mood, vocals, and instruments instead of embedding all details in the prompt string for finer control
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'sad song' or 'rock music' without any descriptive detail or context
Do not skip the confirmation step before calling the generation API, as each request consumes quota
Do not attempt to reproduce or generate copyrighted lyrics, always create original content inspired by the theme

faq.question: What do I need to start using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Node.js installed, the mmx CLI tool installed via npm, and a MiniMax API key from the MiniMax Platform. Run mmx auth login to authenticate before generating music.

faq.question: Can this skill generate music in different languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill detects your language from the first message and responds in that language. Lyrics are generated in your language by default, and you can request lyrics in other languages by specifying them in your prompt.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported for output?
faq.answer: The default output format is MP3. You can also generate WAV or PCM files by using the --format flag. The default bitrate is 256000 and sample rate is 44100.

faq.question: How do I create a cover of an existing song?
faq.answer: Use Cover mode and provide a reference audio file or URL along with a description of the target style. You can also add custom lyrics. The model extracts the original melody and applies your requested style transformation.

faq.question: Can I edit the generated lyrics before creating the song?
faq.answer: Yes. In Advanced Control Mode, you can review, edit, or rewrite lyrics section by section. You can also write your own lyrics and pass them directly via the --lyrics flag.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of API quota?
faq.answer: If your quota is exceeded, the CLI returns exit code 4. You will need to wait for your quota to reset or upgrade your plan. Check your current quota with mmx quota show.
